summary: Repository README defines the package as a runtime prompt plus skills distribution, documents macOS and Windows installation, owner personal-overlay quick installs, and optional memory maintenance setup.
Package verifier gates top-level skill directories, skill frontmatter, runtime prompt presence, routes, lifecycle, memory-system audit, skill-source audit, and commercial-content lint.

Commercial-content audit checks default docs, prompts, and yonghu-preferences files for owner-specific markers, scans all packaged skills for personal markers, and verifies the optional personal overlay preserves owner identity.
Lifecycle audit blocks accepted snapshot writes when unallowlisted suspicious findings are present and accepts clean snapshot refreshes only after scan review.
Memory context resolver reports projectless global preference requests as global writes while keeping explicit project memory routed to project docs.
Global capture writer now writes Candidate rows using the Candidate Preferences schema and creates a clean Change Log section for new profiles.
Project memory audit verifies evidence source hashes and requires docs index coverage for Markdown, JSON, and JSONL durable memory files.

Windows installer requires -Force for owner-specific overlays, backs up destinations before replacement, skips top-level directories missing SKILL.md, and can explicitly enable memory maintenance setup.
Skill-source audit requires inventory coverage, source-review, commercial-use, and redistribution metadata for every packaged skill.
Commercial release builder creates a distribution directory that includes the runtime prompt template and excludes owner-specific overlays and project AGENTS.md.
Skills inventory records source review, commercial-use status, and manual redistribution review status for all 143 packaged skills.
Project memory initializer provides the conversational "初始化本项目" entrypoint and can check, set up, or repair idempotent project memory maintenance.

macOS installer copies packaged skills into the selected Codex home, backs up replaced skills with --force, supports explicit overlays, skips non-skill directories, and can explicitly enable memory maintenance setup.
Rule-governor guidance now treats this repository as a prompt plus skills distribution and keeps runtime prompt templates commercial-safe and version-consistent with user-skills.

Maintenance runner executes daily, weekly, global, and project memory checks, propagates custom CODEX_HOME to child audits, and can write audit reports without automatically writing global Active memory.
Project maintenance setup checks or creates docs/memory/maintenance.json and installs a read-only Git pre-push memory audit hook idempotently.
macOS maintenance setup creates or skips launchd tasks for daily light checks and weekly deep audits, with force and uninstall support.
Windows maintenance setup creates or skips Task Scheduler tasks for daily light checks and weekly deep audits, with force and uninstall support.
Project maintenance policy records enabled daily light audit, weekly deep audit, pre-push audit hook, and explicit no-auto-write/no-auto-delete safeguards.

Runtime prompt template is a compact commercial-safe built-in prompt body for qianzong-NB skills, covering bootstrap, role routing, memory gateway, project initialization, maintenance boundaries, execution rules, and final reporting without installation prose.
Repository distribution boundary changed to skills-only: AGENTS5.3.md and global/project AGENTS.md are not included and must be installed manually on each computer.
